frameworks.name: functional causal flow (FCF)
frameworks.one_line: Delay-embedding-based inference of ensemble causal interactions from resting activity

frameworks.name: delay embedding
frameworks.one_line: State-space reconstruction used to recover functional causal structure

frameworks.name: Granger causality
frameworks.one_line: Alternative connectivity measure benchmarked against FCF

core_question: Can resting functional interactions predict which single-site microstimulations produce large downstream effects on ensemble activity?
key_assumptions: Functional causal interactions inferred at rest generalize to perturbed states
Delay-embedding reconstructs ensemble dynamics from spiking activity
Single-site microstimulation effects can be modeled at the ensemble level
manipulations: Single-site microstimulation in alert monkey prefrontal cortex
FCF compared against Granger causality and transfer entropy
Tested robustness to recording duration and neuron sampling fraction
Validated on ground-truth cortical circuit models
key_findings: FCF predicts how microstimulation alters downstream ensemble spiking in PFC
FCF is robust to noise and recoverable from brief recordings
FCF works with only a small fraction of neurons sampled from the circuit
FCF outperforms Granger causality and transfer entropy in predicting perturbation effects
interpretations: Resting functional interactions encode causal structure that forecasts perturbation efficacy
FCF offers a principled alternative to trial-and-error site selection for targeted stimulation
